completed.title: Expand doctor checks to report source and instance version state
completed.description: Update the CLI doctor command so it reads VERSION and mole.instance.yaml when available, reports both values, and clearly flags missing instance metadata.
acceptance_criteria: A test covers doctor output when mole.instance.yaml exists.
A test covers doctor output when mole.instance.yaml is missing.
doctor output includes source version, instance version, and a clear warning for missing metadata.
Existing doctor scaffold checks still run.

completed.title: Add machine-readable upgrade ownership classes
completed.description: Introduce a conservative manifest that classifies repository paths as safe-copy, merge-carefully, or never-overwrite for future upgrade reporting.
acceptance_criteria: The manifest is documented in a readable format.
Core directories are assigned ownership classes.
User-owned evidence, context, and raw folders are marked never-overwrite.
A test or validation step confirms the manifest can be parsed.

completed.title: Implement check-updates report scaffold
completed.description: Replace the placeholder check-updates output with a report that compares source version to instance metadata and lists likely next actions.
acceptance_criteria: A test covers check-updates output for an up-to-date instance.
A test covers check-updates output when source is newer than the instance.
The report lists safe additions separately from manual review items.
The command remains read-only.

completed.title: Normalize CLI naming and help output
completed.description: Review the CLI help and command output for consistent Mole naming, command examples, and next-step language.
acceptance_criteria: Help output consistently uses Mole and `mole` terminology.
Examples match documented command names.
A test or snapshot protects the main help output.
No command behaviour changes beyond text output.

completed.title: Document the shared-folder team operating model
completed.description: Document how teams can run Mole from a shared cloud-synced folder, including capture versus processing responsibilities and safe provider guidance.
acceptance_criteria: README explains capture versus processing for teams.
README states that only one maintainer or agent should process and promote inbox material at a time.
README describes suggested permissions for raw capture, promoted context, and framework files.
README explains that cloud version history is useful for recovery but not a replacement for careful processing.
README mentions SharePoint/OneDrive, Google Drive for desktop, Dropbox desktop app, and Box Drive as plausible local-file-system options.

completed.title: Make CLI and UI capture filenames collision-resistant
completed.description: Update CLI and local UI capture so repeated or similar notes from multiple contributors do not silently overwrite each other in shared synced folders.
acceptance_criteria: CLI capture filenames include a UTC timestamp and unique suffix.
UI capture filenames include a UTC timestamp and unique suffix.
Tests cover repeated similar notes without filename collision.
Capture refuses or avoids silent overwrite when a target path already exists.

completed.title: Add capture attribution metadata
completed.description: Add contributor attribution fields to raw capture frontmatter so shared inbox material can be traced back to the person or source that supplied it.
acceptance_criteria: CLI capture frontmatter includes captured-by or equivalent contributor metadata.
UI capture frontmatter includes captured-by or equivalent contributor metadata.
Default attribution behaviour is documented for local use.
Tests cover emitted capture attribution metadata.

completed.title: Add inbox processing states
completed.description: Define and document inbox states so team captures can move through new, processing, processed, and archive phases without losing raw inputs.
acceptance_criteria: Docs define new, processing, processed, and archive inbox states.
Agent guidance says not to delete raw inputs before a retrieval receipt exists.
Docs explain how substantive artefacts and weak signals move through the states.
Existing capture paths remain compatible or receive a documented migration path.
validation: rg -n "new/|processing/|processed/|archive/|legacy direct inbox|Never delete raw inputs|retrieval receipt|shared inbox" docs/signal-inbox-workflow.md 0-bootstrap/agents.md README.md

completed.title: Add a simple inbox processing lock and receipt
completed.description: Add a lightweight lock and receipt convention so only one processor claims shared inbox synthesis work at a time.
acceptance_criteria: One processing run can claim the inbox before synthesis begins.
Concurrent processing attempts fail safely with a clear message.
Stale-lock handling is documented.
A processing receipt records who claimed the run, when it started, what was processed, and when it completed.
